perturbation.original_text: Any dispute, controversy or claim between the Parties arising out of, this Agreement or the breach, termination or invalidity thereof, unless settled amicably within twenty (20) days after receipt by one Party of the other Party's request for such amicable settlement, shall be referred by either Party to arbitration in accordance with the UNCITRAL Arbitration Rules then obtaining, including provisions on applicable law. The arbitral tribunal shall have no authority to award punitive damages . In addition, unless otherwise expressly provided in this Agreement, the arbitral tribunal shall have no authority to award interest. The Parties shall be bound by any ar bitration award rendered as a result of such arbitration as the final adjudication of any such controversy, claim or dispute.
perturbation.changed_text: Any dispute, controversy or claim between the Parties arising out of, this Agreement or the breach, termination or invalidity thereof shall be subject to the governing laws of Switzerland. The arbitral tribunal shall have no authority to award punitive damages . In addition, unless otherwise expressly provided in this Agreement, the arbitral tribunal shall have no authority to award interest. Any dispute, controversy or claim between the Parties arising out of, this Agreement or the breach, termination or invalidity thereof, unless settled amicab ly within twenty (20) days after receipt by one Party of the other Party's request for such amicable settlement, shall be referred by either Party to arbitration in accordance with a dispute resolution mechanism to be determined later by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The modification removes the mandatory arbitration clause and replaces it with an agreement to determine the dispute resolution mechanism later. This introduces ambiguity and potential unenforceability of the arbitration agreement, as it is no longer clearly defined or binding.
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 16 of the UNCITRAL Arbitration Rules discusses the competence of the arbitral tribunal to rule on its jurisdiction. By removing the specific reference to UNCITRAL and stating that the arbitration will follow a mechanism to be determined later, the change contradicts the established framework for determining jurisdiction and procedure, potentially leading to a dispute about the arbitration process itself and contradicting the spirit of a binding arbitration agreement.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: s structured so as to be representative of different legal traditions and levels of economic development. As a result of this process, the UNCITRAL texts are widely accepted as offering solutions appropriate to many different countries at different stages of economic development. Library and Research Resources The UNCITRAL Law Library is the specialized trade law library of the United Nations. It was established in 1979 in Vienna as a reference and research library for the Secretariat and participants in intergovernmental meetings convened by UNCITRAL. Case Law (CLOUT) The UNCITRAL Secretariat has established a system for collecting and disseminating information on court decisions and arbitral awards relating to the Conventions and Model Laws that have emanated from the work of the Commission. The purpose of the system is to promote international awareness of the legal texts formulated by the Commission and to facilitate uniform interpretation and application of those texts. Transparency Registry UN Photo by Rick Bajornas Information to be made available to the public under the Rules on Transparency shall be published by a central repository, a function undertaken by the Secretary-General of the United Nations, through the UNCITRAL secretariat. Information shall be published via the UNCITRAL website . UNCITRAL supports the Sustainable Development Goals. UNCITRAL's contribution to the achievement of the Sustainable Development Goals proceeds on several fronts and touches upon a number of different areas. Learn more here . Sessions Commission 7–23 July 2025, Vienna Working Group I 29 September-3 October 2025 , Vienna (tentative) Working Group II - Dispute Settlement 13-17 October 2025, Vienna (tentative) Working Group III - Investor-State Dispute Settlement Reform 22-26 September 2025, Vienna (tentative) Working Group IV - Electronic Commerce 18–22 November 2024, Vienna 24-28 March 2025, New York 20-24 October 2025, Vienna (tentative) Working Group V - Insolvency Law 16-20 December 2024, Vienna 12-16 May 2025, New York 8-12 December 2025, Vienna (tentative) Working Group VI - Negotiable Cargo Documents 15-19 December 2025, Vienna (tentative)
perturbation.justification: The original contract mandated arbitration under UNCITRAL rules, but the changed text removes this mandate, replacing it with a vague agreement to determine the dispute resolution mechanism later. The LLM Law explanation indicates that removing the specific reference to UNCITRAL contradicts the established framework for determining jurisdiction. Scraped Law Snippet 1 mentions UNCITRAL's dispute settlement working group, highlighting its role in international dispute resolution, which is undermined by the change. The perturbation explanation confirms that this creates ambiguity and potential unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient acknowledges that UNHCR hereto makes no any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information .
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient acknowledges that UNHCR hereto makes no any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information . The Recipient shall make no public announcement regarding this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By placing the clause about no public announcement (which relates to public relations) within the disclaimer of accuracy of information, it buries the clause where someone looking for PR restrictions would not normally find it.
perturbation.law_explanation: The principle of fair contract terms requires that important clauses are conspicuous and easily understandable. Placing a significant restriction like prohibiting public announcements within a disclaimer regarding the accuracy of information buries it where it wouldn't be reasonably expected, potentially making it unenforceable due to lack of conspicuousness and fair presentation.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: contract of adhesion | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you contract of adhesion See Adhesion contract . Wex COMMERCE commercial activities business law commercial law contracts LIFE EVENTS accidents & injuries (tort law) mass tort litigation product liability law standards of tort liability tort damages wex definitions Wex Toolbox
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that the clause prohibiting public announcement was placed in a section regarding the accuracy of information. The law explanation states the principle of fair contract terms requires that important clauses are conspicuous and easily understandable. The 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction' definition says that a contradiction arises when a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret. The added clause, regarding public announcements, is buried in an inappropriate section of the contract, hindering its discoverability. This supports the argument that a contradiction exists based on structural flaws affecting clarity and enforceability. Scraped law snippet 2 mentions 'contract of adhesion', which can be relevant where there's unequal bargaining power and potentially unfair terms. While not directly stating the illegality, it indicates potential issues with burying important clauses.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information in any form and any medium, including all copies thereof, disclosed to the Recipient shall be returned to UNHCR or destr oyed: (a) if a business relationship is not entered into with UNHCR on or before the date which is three (3) months after the date both Parties have signed the Agreement; or (b) promptly upon request by the UNHCR at any time.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information in any form and any medium, including all copies thereof, disclosed to the Recipient shall be returned to UNHCR or destr oyed promptly upon request by the UNHCR at any time. A business relationship between both parties must be established within three months.
perturbation.explanation: The modification removes the automatic requirement to return or destroy confidential information if a business relationship is *not* formed within three months. It replaces that with an unconditional requirement for a business relationship. This creates a contradiction: The agreement now implies a business relationship *must* be entered, which may conflict with the parties' actual intentions and obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_law: General Contract Law - Offer and Acceptance
perturbation.law_explanation: General contract law requires a clear offer and acceptance to form a binding agreement. By implying a mandatory business relationship, the modified text creates an obligation that might not reflect the true intent or acceptance of both parties. This contradicts the principle that parties must freely consent to the terms of a contract.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: offer | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you offer Offer is part of contract negotiations where a party agrees to do or not do something in exchange for consideration . An offer must be stated and delivered in a way that would lead a reasonable person to expect a binding contract to arise from its acceptance . An offer gives the offeree the ability to accept and form an enforceable contract. When a valid offer is made, an acceptance by the offeree creates a binding contract. An offeror generally cannot retract the offer once the other party has accepted. A court may not find a binding contract regardless, though, if the negotiation did not involve consideration, involves an illusory promise , or if the offeror did not have the capacity to enter into the contract. However, a contract offer can be withdrawn by notification to the offeree of the intent to withdraw the offer before the offeree has accepted. Offerors typically can withdraw an offer before acceptance in any contract negotiation unless the parties have created an enforceable option contract . If the offeree rejects the offer, the offer can no longer be accepted by the offeree. An offer can also be made in the form of a counteroffer . The offeree that receives the initial offer can make a new offer of their own instead of accepting the initial offer. This counteroffer acts as a rejection of the initial offer and creates an entirely new offer that can be accepted or rejected. [Last reviewed in August of 2023 by the Wex Definitions Team ] Wex COMMERCE commercial activities business law contracts wex definitions business sectors commercial transactions Wex Toolbox
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a contradiction by removing the condition for returning confidential information if no business relationship is established within three months, and instead implies a business relationship *must* be formed. This creates a conflict because it potentially forces a business relationship that may not be intended by both parties. According to the provided law snippet, a contract requires an offer and acceptance that reflects the parties' true intent. The altered text creates an obligation for a business relationship that may not be the 'true intent' or 'acceptance of both parties,' potentially contradicting the principle of free consent.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose for which it was disclosed ;
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient shall use the Confidential Information solely for marketing purposes ;
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original intention of the confidentiality agreement, which was to protect the Discloser's information. Changing the permitted use to "marketing purposes" allows the Recipient to exploit the confidential information for their own gain, potentially harming the Discloser. By changing the 'purpose for which it was disclosed' to 'marketing purposes' it goes against the basic contract law that confidential information is to be used ONLY for the limited scope in the original text.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Breach of Contract - Misappropriation of Trade Secrets
perturbation.law_explanation: The Uniform Trade Secrets Act defines misappropriation as the use of a trade secret (which confidential information can be) without consent. Allowing the Recipient to use the information for marketing purposes is a direct misappropriation, violating the Discloser's rights and the intended purpose of the confidentiality agreement.

perturbation.justification: The original text restricts the use of confidential information to the purpose for which it was disclosed. The changed text broadens this to 'marketing purposes,' which directly opposes the initial agreement's limited scope. The Law Explanation corroborates this by stating that using confidential information without consent constitutes misappropriation, and by using it for marketing purposes directly allows it to be used without the Discloser's consent.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient agrees to indem nify UNHCR in respect of any expenses, losses, damages, costs, claims or liability UNHCR may suffer or incur as a result of an act or omission by the Recipient or its 4 employees, consultants and agents in connection with the Confidential Information and the Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient agrees to indem nify UNHCR in respect of any expenses, losses, damages, costs, claims or liability UNHCR may suffer or incur as a result of an act or omission by the Recipient or its 4 employees, consultants and agents in connection with the Confidential Information and the Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement. However, the Recipient's liability will never extend beyond $100.
perturbation.explanation: The modification adds a clause limiting the Recipient's liability to $100. This creates a contradiction because the original indemnification clause implies potentially unlimited liability for damages resulting from a breach. The cap effectively negates the purpose of indemnification, which is to fully compensate the injured party.
perturbation.law_citation: General Contract Law Principles on Indemnification
perturbation.law_explanation: Indemnification aims to protect a party from financial loss. By capping the liability at $100, the added clause severely undermines the indemnification provision, making it practically worthless and contradicting the fundamental principle that the indemnifying party should bear the full cost of the covered loss.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: indemnify | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you indemnify To indemnify, also known as indemnity or indemnification, means compensating a person for damages or losses they have incurred or will incur related to a specified accident, incident, or event. Typically, parties make a written agreement in which one party (indemnitor) promises to indemnify the other party (indemnitee) for future specified losses. The more common form of an indemnification agreement is the insurance policy. This often happens when an insurance company, as part of an individual's insurance policy, agrees to indemnify the insured person for losses that the insured person incurred due to accident or property damage. In this sort of agreement, party A would hire insurance company B. If party A and party C are involved in some incident that results in property or personal damage (ex: a car accident), insurance company B would assume any liability for which Party A might be responsible. After that, insurance company B would typically sue party C to recover damages. See Hercules Inc. et al v. United States, 516 U.S. 417 (1996). [Last reviewed in March of 2023 by the Wex Definitions Team ] Keywords contract contract law liability Wex accidents and injuries COMMERCE commercial activities insurance PROPERTY contracts wex definitions business sectors commercial transactions legal education and practice Wex Toolbox
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a cap on liability ($100), which directly contradicts the principle of indemnification. Indemnification, as described in Scraped Law Snippet 1, means "compensating a person for damages or losses they have incurred or will incur related to a specified accident, incident, or event." The added clause severely undermines the indemnification provision, making it practically worthless. A cap of $100 would likely not fully compensate UNHCR for any significant losses, thus contradicting the fundamental purpose of indemnification.
